current_fact.fact_text: In 1770, Lieutenant James Cook became the first recorded European to sight the eastern coast of what is now Australia. His observation from the deck of HMS Endeavour foreshadowed profound cultural and ecological changes to an entire continent.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

current_fact.match_reason: An 18th‑century voyage of discovery aligns with a modern sailing‑boat soundtrack, both shaped by life at sea and new horizons.

queue.fact_text: In 1713, with no surviving sons, Holy Roman Emperor Charles VI issued the Pragmatic Sanction to allow his daughter Maria Theresa to inherit the Habsburg lands. This attempt to rewrite the rules of succession would later ignite wars over a woman’s right to rule.

episode.title: Morning Drops #36 - Women of Rock
episode.description: A tribute to the women who shaped the sound of rock, the ones who picked up a guitar and refused to ask for permission. The conversation unfolds in Italian, led by Gaia and her father. A dialogue between generations and a reminder that the pulse of rock has always belonged to anyone brave enough to play it loud. Distortion, rhythm, freedom.
songs: Joan Jett & The Blackhearts - I Love Rock 'n Roll

queue.match_reason: A royal decree reshaping power for a future empress resonates with an episode celebrating women who claimed space in a male‑dominated rock world.

queue.fact_text: On 19 April 65 AD, the freedman Milichus betrayed the Pisonian conspiracy to assassinate Emperor Nero, exposing one of Rome’s most dramatic plots. His decision shifted the balance between power and resistance in the imperial court overnight.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A conspiracy, betrayal, and oppressive power in ancient Rome mirror the episode’s themes of resistance, uprising, and speaking against authority.

queue.fact_text: In 2008, a fire at the Ultratumba nightclub in Quito, Ecuador, killed 19 people and injured at least 24. The tragedy highlighted how quickly celebration can turn to catastrophe when safety measures are overlooked in crowded urban spaces.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

queue.match_reason: A painful reminder of fragility and loss is balanced by an episode about storms passing and finding cautious hope after dark moments.

queue.fact_text: Also in 1971, Sierra Leone declared itself a republic, replacing Queen Elizabeth II with Siaka Stevens as president. This shift marked a new chapter in the country’s post-colonial search for political self-determination.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: A nation shifting its political identity aligns with an episode about movement, perspective shifts, and quietly transformative journeys.

queue.fact_text: In 1942, during World War II, the Majdan-Tatarski ghetto was established in German-occupied Poland between the Lublin Ghetto and a Majdanek subcamp. It became another node in the brutal machinery of segregation, forced labour, and extermination.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: A somber historical moment calls for a reflective, grounded episode about pain, endurance, and slowly rebuilding after devastation.

queue.fact_text: In 1927, American actress Mae West was sentenced to ten days in jail for her play "Sex," deemed obscene by authorities. The scandal only amplified her fame and challenged the era’s strict ideas about morality, female sexuality, and censorship.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: Mae West’s refusal to fit into polite boxes matches an episode dedicated to breaking labels and embracing unapologetic self-definition.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1925, the Chilean football club Colo-Colo was founded in Santiago by David Arellano and teammates who had broken away from another club. Named after a Mapuche leader, it grew into the country’s most successful and passionately supported team.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

queue.match_reason: A new football club born from a split, full of movement and collective energy, fits an upbeat 'on the go' mix made for crowds and momentum.

queue.fact_text: In 1976, a violent F5 tornado struck near Brownwood, Texas, injuring 11 people. Remarkably, two people were thrown roughly 1,000 yards by the tornado and survived without injuries, a near-impossible brush with nature’s raw power.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: The sudden, overwhelming force of an F5 tornado parallels the episode’s 'electric shock' of high‑voltage guitars and adrenaline.

queue.fact_text: On 19 April 1956, Hollywood star Grace Kelly married Prince Rainier III of Monaco, transforming from Oscar‑winning actress to European princess. Their wedding blurred the line between cinema and royalty, becoming one of the most-watched fairy-tale events of the century.

episode.title: Sedna FM #04 - Queen of Spades
episode.description: Tuning in from the planet of irrational love. Exploring love's most vulnerable side — the kind that awakens the child within us. A diverse music selection ranging from electric blues to downtempo, soul, and jazz. A tribute to the feeling that has moved artists to create the most beautiful works. Love makes us vulnerable, and there is no art without vulnerability.
songs: Alabama Shakes - Gimme All Your Love
Queens of the stone age - Make it wit chu
Etta James - I'd Rather Go Blind
Eric Clapton - Have you ever loved a woman

queue.match_reason: A royal love story at the edge of cinema and monarchy fits an episode dedicated to irrational love, glamour, and vulnerability.

queue.fact_text: In 1810, the people of Caracas deposed colonial governor Vicente Emparán and established a local junta, a key step in Venezuela’s path toward independence. It was a moment when a city’s plaza became the stage for rewriting who held power.

episode.title: Morning Drops #26 - Canserbero
episode.description: Yasmin guides us through the story of Canserbero, the Venezuelan rapper who reshaped Latin American hip hop with his raw voice and deeply reflective lyrics. A journey into his music, his vision, and the legacy of an artist who transformed personal and social struggles into powerful storytelling.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como una película y su tragedia, comedia y ficción

queue.match_reason: The first push toward Venezuelan self-rule resonates with a modern Venezuelan voice who turned political and social struggles into rap.

queue.fact_text: On 19 April 1782, John Adams secured Dutch recognition of the United States as an independent nation, and his purchased house in The Hague became the first American embassy. It was diplomacy done on a human scale, turning an ordinary home into a symbol of a new country.

episode.title: Morning Drops #41 - Sedna's friends: Marina
episode.description:
Today's Morning Drops episode feels like an easy coffee with someone you instantly get along with. It also introduces Sedna's Friends, a new sub-format where friends are invited to drink coffee and share the music they love.

Gaia invites her friend Marina, artist and aspiring singer, to share music, small thoughts, and that comfortable kind of conversation between good friends. They met by chance at a party and connected right away through music, creativity, and a healthy dose of sarcasm.

This episode sounds exactly like that: relaxed, honest, and a bit spontaneous. Press play, get comfortable, and enjoy the listen.

queue.match_reason: A simple house turned embassy echoes an episode where a casual coffee between friends becomes a meaningful meeting point across borders.

queue.fact_text: In 1809, during the War of the Fifth Coalition, an Austrian corps was defeated by the Duchy of Warsaw at Raszyn, while the Austrian main army lost to Marshal Davout at Teugen-Hausen. Within days, these clashes helped secure a wider French victory across Bavaria and Poland.

episode.title: Morning Drops #46 - Fog and Fire
episode.description: In today’s Morning Drops, Gaia brings us on a passport-free trip between two Italian energies: the foggy stillness of the North and the volcanic pulse of the South. Throughout the journey, Gaia shares four of her favourite Italian songwriters. Grab your coffee and tune into the radio show from another planet!
songs: Edoardo Bennato - La Torre Di Babele
Lucio Dalla - Com'è Profondo il Mare

queue.match_reason: A campaign of smoke, chaos, and sudden clashes suits an episode themed around contrasts—fog and fire, stillness and eruption.

queue.fact_text: On 19 April 1987, The Simpsons made their first appearance as rough, one‑minute shorts on The Tracey Ullman Show. These simple interludes would evolve into one of the longest‑running and most influential animated series in television history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - secret_mp3_player_1
episode.description: Inspired by a throwback MP3 player on Instagram, Yasmin and Gaia take you on a nostalgic journey to the early 2000s. LimeWire, eMule, guilty-pleasure playlists — remember those? Hit play and relive the songs that shaped a generation.

queue.match_reason: Early, scrappy Simpsons shorts that later defined a generation match an episode steeped in pop‑culture nostalgia and formative screens.

queue.fact_text: In 2001, Space Shuttle Endeavour’s STS-100 mission not only carried Canadarm2, but also symbolized international cooperation on the International Space Station, with contributions from NASA, ESA, CSA, and other partners. It turned low Earth orbit into a truly shared laboratory.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A collaborative mission in orbit echoes a 'collective voyage through sound' that brings together many cultures and musical roots.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1984, Australia also officially adopted green and gold as its national colours, drawn from its native flora like the golden wattle. These hues now unify everything from sports uniforms to cultural branding with a distinctly earthy palette.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: Green and gold evoke quiet landscapes and sunlit fields, fitting a mellow, nature‑tinted 'slow planet' soundtrack under open skies.

queue.fact_text: On 19 April 1971, as Sierra Leone became a republic, Siaka Stevens was sworn in as its first executive president. His long, controversial rule would shape the country’s political landscape for decades, for better and for worse.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: The rise of a powerful, contested leader in a young republic suits an episode about a fiercely political artist whose lyrics dissect power and identity.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1809, the same campaign that saw Raszyn and Teugen-Hausen revealed how quickly fortunes could shift in the Napoleonic Wars. Within four days, coordinated maneuvers, logistics, and timing tipped a multi‑front struggle into a decisive French victory.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: A four‑day campaign that set the tone for a wider war pairs with Sedna’s explosive first episode—the 'bomb' that defined everything that followed.

published.fact_text: In 1971, the Soviet Union launched Salyut 1, the world’s first space station, turning science fiction visions of orbital habitats into reality. Though its operational life was short, Salyut 1 pioneered the idea of humans living and working for extended periods in space.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A literal 'space shift' in human history pairs with an episode about reimagining empty space as a place to create.

published.fact_text: On 19 April 2001, Space Shuttle Endeavour launched on mission STS-100, delivering the Canadian-built Canadarm2 to the International Space Station. This giant robotic arm became the station’s mechanical “hand,” crucial for assembly, maintenance, and capturing visiting spacecraft.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

published.match_reason: A robotic arm assembling a modular space habitat fits a layered, slowly building mix that “constructs” a musical journey piece by piece.

published.fact_text: In 1975, India’s first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched from the Soviet launch site Kapustin Yar. Built almost entirely in India, it marked a bold technological leap for a newly independent nation beginning its own journey into space.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

published.match_reason: Both tell stories of postcolonial nations asserting their identity on a global stage—one through satellites, the other through revolutionary music.

published.fact_text: On 19 April 2021, NASA’s Ingenuity helicopter achieved the first powered, controlled flight on another planet, rising into the thin Martian atmosphere. Its 39.1‑second hop was the extraterrestrial echo of the Wright brothers’ flight on Earth.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description:
Cosmic Monday is a Morning Drops episode built around four Italian dance records, tracing how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language.
Gaia moves through four tracks like chapters: groove first, then machines, then that bright, futuristic feeling you only get when the bassline is doing the talking. It's not nostalgia, exactly. It's more like returning to a sound that still works — especially on mornings when you need a little extra energy, but you don't want anything aggressive.
Take it as a slow transmission. Let it carry you into the day.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

published.match_reason: A tiny helicopter flying on Mars feels like the ultimate 'cosmic Monday'—machines, rhythm, and a futuristic leap into the week.

published.fact_text: In 1818, Augustin-Jean Fresnel submitted his groundbreaking “Note on the Theory of Diffraction,” ending with what are now known as the Fresnel integrals. His work helped prove that light behaves as a wave, reshaping our understanding of optics and paving the way for modern lenses and lasers.

episode.title: Morning Drops #25 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

published.match_reason: Fresnel’s interference patterns and wave theories parallel trip-hop’s layered, overlapping textures and cinematic waves of sound.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1984, Australia formally adopted “Advance Australia Fair” as its national anthem, and chose green and gold as its official national colours. The decision symbolically distanced the country from its colonial past while embracing its unique identity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

published.match_reason: A national anthem is designed to be a 'timeless legacy,' echoing the episode’s focus on enduring, classic sounds that define identity.

From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
